analysis.consistency: High. All fields are present and logically consistent for a 5e spell entry.
analysis.quality: High. The description is detailed, clearly outlining mechanical benefits (disadvantage on attacks, immunity/advantage on saves against specific conditions) against defined creature types.

mechanics.components_check: Material component (M) lists consumables (holy water OR powdered silver/iron), which correctly implies consumption, noted in the description.
mechanics.duration: Concentration up to 10 minutes (Standard for protective concentration buffs).
mechanics.effects_summary: Target gains defensive benefits against aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead. Benefits include: 1. Disadvantage on attack rolls from these types. 2. Immunity to being charmed, frightened, or possessed by these types. 3. Advantage on new saving throws vs. existing effects.
mechanics.class_availability: Cleric, Paladin, Warlock, Wizard. Appropriate distribution for an Abjuration spell offering specific protection.

analysis.archival_notes: Mechanics are clear. The spell effectively counters many common planar threats and undead found across various CR ranges.
